prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

(allocate_workflow_resource primary_participant_instance resource_token_a)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(attach_primary_artifact primary_participant_instance signal_channel_alpha supporting_artifact)

(release_operation_type_binding secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)

(bind_operation_type_to_workflow primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(verify_output_bundle consolidated_output_bundle primary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(restore_primary_artifact primary_participant_instance signal_channel_alpha standard_operation_type supporting_artifact)
(mark_secondary_participant_complete secondary_participant_instance consolidated_output_bundle)
(mark_primary_participant_complete primary_participant_instance consolidated_output_bundle)
(initialize_workflow_instance lead_orchestrator)
(allocate_workflow_resource lead_orchestrator resource_token_c)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow lead_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding lead_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_attachment_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle)
(bind_policy_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator policy_a)
(certify_policy_binding lead_orchestrator attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle standard_operation_type policy_a)
(bind_execution_parameter_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(bind_capability_to_workflow lead_orchestrator processing_capability)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_a lead_orchestrator execution_parameter_main processing_capability attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle)
(bind_notification_template_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_d lead_orchestrator notification_template_main attachment_a consolidated_output_bundle)
(attach_metadata_to_orchestrator lead_orchestrator metadata_a)
(validate_orchestrator_enrichment lead_orchestrator primary_participant_instance secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type metadata_a)
(certify_orchestrator_finalization lead_orchestrator)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance lead_orchestrator audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_orchestrator lead_orchestrator resource_token_c audit_token_main)
(initialize_workflow_instance support_orchestrator)
(allocate_workflow_resource support_orchestrator resource_token_c)
(release_operation_type_binding lead_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(confirm_workflow_operation_binding support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_attachment_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle)
(bind_policy_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator policy_b)
(certify_policy_binding support_orchestrator attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle standard_operation_type policy_b)
(unbind_execution_parameter_from_orchestrator lead_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(bind_execution_parameter_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator execution_parameter_main)
(release_capability_binding lead_orchestrator processing_capability)
(bind_capability_to_workflow support_orchestrator processing_capability)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_a support_orchestrator execution_parameter_main processing_capability attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle)
(unbind_notification_template_from_orchestrator lead_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(bind_notification_template_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator notification_template_main)
(promote_orchestrator_checkpoint_d support_orchestrator notification_template_main attachment_b consolidated_output_bundle)
(attach_metadata_to_orchestrator support_orchestrator metadata_b)
(validate_orchestrator_enrichment support_orchestrator primary_participant_instance secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type metadata_b)
(certify_orchestrator_finalization support_orchestrator)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance primary_participant_instance audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_primary_participant primary_participant_instance resource_token_a audit_token_main)
(release_operation_type_binding support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow secondary_participant_instance standard_operation_type)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance secondary_participant_instance audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_secondary_participant secondary_participant_instance resource_token_b audit_token_main)
(bind_operation_type_to_workflow support_orchestrator standard_operation_type)
(bind_audit_token_to_workflow_instance support_orchestrator audit_token_main standard_operation_type)
(finalize_orchestrator support_orchestrator resource_token_c audit_token_main)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex multi-step workflow execution with primary_participant_instance, secondary_participant_instance, lead_orchestrator, support_orchestrator, and various resources.


## Examining Workflow Components
- The problem involves a complex workflow with multiple participants and orchestrators.
- Key roles include primary_participant_instance, secondary_participant_instance, lead_orchestrator, and support_orchestrator, each likely handling specific tasks.  
- Various resources are also part of the workflow, suggesting dependencies or shared assets.  
- The goal is to execute a multi-step process, implying coordination and sequencing among components.

## Examining Workflow Components
- Finalization requires audit_stage_open, involving workflow_resource_binding and workflow_audit_link, and releases resources and audit tokens.
- To achieve audit_stage_open, completion_signaled is needed, along with workflow_operation_binding and audit_token_available.

## Detailing Participant Processes
- Participants signal completion via mark_primary_participant_complete and mark_secondary_participant_complete, requiring primed and committed states.
- Priming and committing can involve emitting signals, attaching artifacts, or restoring from buffered states, with flexibility in the process.

## Managing Shared Resources
- The supporting_artifact is single, so only one participant can attach it; others may emit signals without consuming it.
- Output bundles are shared, with variants of consolidate_output_bundle (a, b, c, d) depending on signal and commitment status.

## Analyzing Orchestrator Roles
- Orchestrators lack direct channel bindings but link to consolidated_output_bundle via orchestrator_output_link.
- Binding attachments to orchestrators requires context_enabled, output_prepared, and bundle_verified, setting attachment bindings and releasing availability.

## Exploring Participant Coordination  
- Binding operation types (OT) and capabilities (Cap) to participants is necessary for priming and emitting signals, but OT is shared and can't bind to both simultaneously.  
- Releasing OT from one participant (P) makes it available for the other (S), but verifying output bundles later requires OT still bound to P, creating a timing conflict.  

## Investigating Signal and Artifact Use  
- Attaching artifacts primes participants without emitting signals, buffering instead, and consuming the single supporting_artifact.  
- One participant can attach the artifact (buffered, primed, not committed), while the other emits a signal with capability binding (emitted, committed), enabling variant b or c consolidation.  

## Resolving Verification Challenges  
- Verification needs the operation binding on the primary participant, but releasing it for the secondary causes issues, as consolidate_output_bundle requires both primed and committed.  
- A potential solution is verifying output bundles before releasing OT, ensuring the binding remains for verification while allowing participant coordination.

## Extending Participant Coordination
- Releasing the operation binding (OT) from the primary participant (P) after attaching artifacts makes OT available for the secondary participant (S).
- After using OT for S (emitting signals and priming), OT can be rebound to P for verification, ensuring all steps work sequentially.

## Refining Verification Process
- Rebinding OT to P after consolidation allows verification of the output bundle, satisfying the need for an active binding during verification.
- This approach resolves timing conflicts, enabling both participants to use OT while maintaining system integrity for marking completion.

## Extending Orchestrator Setup  
- Initializing lead orchestrator (L) and binding resource token C (RtC) prepares it for further operations.  
- Binding operation type (OT) and confirming workflow operation ensures context for L, enabling attachment and policy binding.  

## Binding Attachments and Policies  
- Attaching artifact A to L links it to the output bundle, but requires certifying via policy due to existing marker_a.  
- Binding policy A to L and certifying it sets stage_a, making L approval-ready for promotion.

## Attaching Metadata and Validation  
- Attaching metadata A to lead orchestrator (L) links it, with operation binding (OT) still active.  
- Validating enrichment with primary and secondary participants sets validation_passed, enabling finalization steps.  

## Finalizing Orchestrator Status  
- Certifying finalization sets orchestrator_finalized and completion_signaled for L, but not the broader finalized status.  
- Finalizing requires additional audit steps, as completion_signaled alone doesn’t achieve the goal of finalized workflow instances.  

## Preparing for Audit Process  
- Binding audit token to L opens the audit stage, linking it and making the token unavailable.  
- Finalization needs audit linkage and resource binding, ensuring resources like RtC can be released afterward.

## Finalizing Participant Audits
- Binding audit token to primary participant (P) opens the audit stage, linking it and making the token unavailable temporarily.
- Finalizing P releases resource token A (RtA) and audit token, making both available for subsequent steps.

## Managing Operation Bindings
- Releasing operation type (OT) from support orchestrator (U) makes it available for rebinding to secondary participant (S) for audit.
- Rebinding OT to S ensures it meets audit requirements, enabling finalization and releasing resource token B (RtB).

## Completing Orchestrator Finalization
- Rebinding OT to U and binding audit token prepares for finalization, releasing resource token C (RtC) afterward.
- All participants (P, S, L, U) are now finalized, completing the workflow process.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
